actionLibraryList.title: The World's Greatest Stretch​
actionLibraryList.context: Keep your core engaged and pelvis in a neutral position.
actionLibraryList.showDetails:
[{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749634409876.jpg","context":"1.Place both hands on the floor shoulder-width apart and bend one elbow.
2.Keep the back leg straight.
3.Keep the front thigh parallel to the floor."},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749634414396.jpg","context":"1.Keep the knee behind the toes.
2.Rotate the upper body toward the front leg and extend the same-side arm upward."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Inhale to lengthen the spine, exhale to deepen the stretch.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: You’ll feel a stretch in the hips, thoracic spine, and shoulders.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Avoid compensatory rotation in the lumbar spine or inward collapse of the knee.

actionLibraryList.title: In-place Inchworm
actionLibraryList.context: Ensure the entire palm remains in contact with the floor.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749700102676.jpg","context":"1.Always maintain the feeling of pushing your heels back into the ground.\n2.Keep your knees fully extended at all times.\n3.Ensure the entire palm remains in contact with the floor."}]
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Focus on the stretch in your legs

actionLibraryList.title: Aerobic Skiing (bent arms)
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Stand in the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart, knees and hips slightly bent, and lean forward slightly with your hands gripping the handles.
2.Keep your arms bent, initiating the pull by engaging your core. Swing your arms back past your thighs.
3.Bend and extend the knees in a fluid motion, driving the handles downward by hinging at the hips. Exhale as you pull, and inhale as you return to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice1Txt: Adjust the slider to the highest position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, hinge at your hips with a slight bend in your knees, and grip the handles with both hands.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice3Txt: Knees bend and extend in a fluid motion, while your hips sit back and down.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice4Txt: Keep your arms bent and use your body's explosive power to pull the handles down.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice5Txt: Engage your core throughout the movement. Exhale as you pull, and inhale as you return to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: As you pull the handles down to your hips, engage your back and lower body muscles.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"context":"1.Keep your back straight.\n2.Hold the ski handles with both hands, keeping them no higher than shoulder level.\n3.Bend your elbows and pull the ropes down to your sides with force.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750671815181.jpg"},{"context":"1.Keep your arms close to your body and maintain good balance throughout.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750671818931.jpg"}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Engage your core throughout the movement. Exhale while pulling, inhale when returning to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: As you pull the handles down toward your hips, activate your back and lower body muscles.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Keep your arms bent and use your body's explosive power to pull the handles down.

actionLibraryList.title: Barbell Stiff-Leg Deadlift
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, grip the barbell with an overhand grip slightly wider than shoulder-width.
2.Engage your core, retract your shoulder blades, and keep your back straight.
3.Hinge forward from the hips, maintaining a straight line between your torso and legs.
4.Lower the barbell slowly along the front of your thighs to mid-shin or your stretch limit.
5.Drive through your glutes and hamstrings to pull back to the starting position with control.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749438751670.jpg","context":"1.Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding the barbell with both hands in a neutral grip, slightly wider than shoulder-width."},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749438774602.jpg","context":"1.Keep your back straight and control the movement by slightly bending your knees and pushing your hips back.\n2.Lean your torso forward, lowering the barbell along your legs to mid-calf or until you reach your maximum stretch."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Inhale as you lower the barbell, exhale as you rise back up.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Keep your knees stable—avoid forward travel or excessive bending.

actionLibraryList.title: Barbell Alternating Reverse Lunge
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Step one leg back and lower into a lunge until both knees are at about 90°.
2.Keep your torso upright, avoiding leaning forward or swaying side to side.
3.Ensure the front knee does not go past your toes.
4.Push back to standing, then switch legs and repeat.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice1Txt: Set the cable to the floor position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Stand with your feet hip-width apart, keep your shoulders back and down, and engage your core.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice3Txt: Take a step backward, bend both knees to lower your body into a lunge, and return to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice4Txt: Perform the movement slowly and with control, keeping your core engaged to maintain balance.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice5Txt: Exhale while exerting force, inhale while returning.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: Targets the glutes, quads, and hamstrings.
actionLibraryList.showDetails:
[{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750231087941.jpg","context":"1.Stand with your feet hip-width apart.
2.Keep your shoulders back and down.
3.Engage your core."},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750231098176.jpg","context":"1.The knee should nearly touch the floor.
2.Keep your torso straight."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Inhale as you squat, exhale as you stand.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Targets the glutes, quads, and hamstrings.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Perform the movement slowly and with control, keeping your core engaged to maintain balance.

actionLibraryList.title: Laying Straight Leg Figure Eight
actionLibraryList.context: Lie on your back with legs straight and arms by your sides or under your hips for support. Engage your core, press your back into the ground, and lift your legs 30 degrees. Slowly trace an "8" with your feet, keeping your hips stable. Avoid lifting your lower back or swinging. Maintain steady breathing. This targets core strength and lower body control.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750572963952.jpg","context":"1.Keep your legs together and straight."},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750572967894.jpg","context":"1.Slowly trace a figure-\"8\" pattern in the air with both legs."}]
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Avoid lifting your lower back or swinging.

actionLibraryList.title: Dual-Handle Single-Leg Romanian Deadlift
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Set the machine handles on the floor mounts.
2.Grab a handle in each hand, holding with your arms straight and hands in front of your hips.
3.Lift one foot off the floor with your knee partially bent.
4.Keep your shoulders back, core engaged, and lead knee slightly bent.
5.Start driving your hips and kicking the lifted leg backwards.
6.Do not allow the lead knee to bend too much or the upper body to lose posture.
7.Keep your hips parallel to the floor throughout the movement.
8.Continue bending and feel the stretch in your hamstrings.
9.Pause slightly at the bottom position, then drive your hips forward and return to standing.
actionLibraryList.actionNameVoiceTxt: Dual Handle Single Leg Romanian Deadlift
actionLibraryList.guideVoice1Txt: Lock the pulley to the floor, ensuring two handles are attached.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Hinge at your hips and lower your torso. Extend your leg backward and reach for balance with your support leg.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice4Txt: Engage your core to stabilize your balance.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice5Txt: Keep your breath steady. Exhale as you lower your torso, and inhale as you raise it back up.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: Briefly pause at the bottom, feel a deep stretch in your support leg, then squeeze your glutes and raise your torso back to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749437298075.jpg","context":"1.Keep your back upright and your glutes engaged."},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749437305516.jpg","context":"1.Lengthen your spine, with your eyes looking at the ground.\n2.Keep your core tight to stabilize your balance.\n3.Feel the stretch coming from your hamstrings."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Keep your breath smooth throughout. Inhale as you lower down, exhale as you rise up.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Hinge at your hips to fully stretch your hamstrings.

actionLibraryList.title: Seated Dual-Handle Low to High Chest Fly
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Sit on the bench with your back firmly against the backrest.
2.Grip the lower handles on each side with your arms hanging naturally at your sides.
3.Engage your chest muscles to bring your arms forward and upward in an arc, finishing around chin or nose level.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750393021921.jpg","context":"1.Keep your core tight with your back against the seat.\n2.Grip the handles with your arms hanging naturally at your sides."},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750393026266.jpg","context":"1.Keep your wrists aligned with your forearms."}]
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Engage the upper part of the pectoralis major.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Avoid fully locking out the arms.

actionLibraryList.title: Leg Curl Cross-Flatbench
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Adjust the rope to the 9th hole position.
2.Place the flat bench horizontally on the footboard, and attach the ankle strap to one leg.
3.Get into a four-point kneeling position, with your arms and thighs parallel and perpendicular to the floor. Place the strap behind you on the flat bench, supporting your body on your elbows.
4.Add weight, engage your leg muscles, and curl your lower leg upwards. Avoid using your lower back for assistance.
5.Breathe with your diaphragm, exhaling during exertion.
6.Tighten your core.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"context":"1.Set up the bench across from the machine, and put the strap on one leg.\n2.Get on all fours, with arms and legs parallel to each other and perpendicular to the floor; place the knee of the working leg on the bench and lower onto your elbows.\n3.Add weight, engage your leg muscles, and curl your lower leg upwards. Avoid using your lower back for assistance.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1682501571795.jpg"}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Breathe with your diaphragm and exhale during each concentric phase.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Add weight, engage your leg muscles, and curl your lower leg upwards. Avoid using your lower back for assistance.

actionLibraryList.title: Standing Barbell Calf Raise
actionLibraryList.context:
1.tand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell with a neutral grip.
2.Engage your core and, in a controlled manner, raise your heels by pushing through the ankles until you reach the highest point.
3.Hold the top position for 1–2 seconds, keeping your calves fully contracted.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1748934010205.jpg","context":"1.Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, grip the barbell with an overhand grip, engage your core, and keep your torso upright."},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1748934014024.jpg","context":"1.With control, lift your heels off the ground and rise onto your toes as high as possible, squeezing your calves."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale as you lift, inhale as you lower.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Control the movement speed and avoid using momentum.

actionLibraryList.title: Pogo Jumps
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Generate explosive power primarily through ankle articulation with quick, short-range jumps.
2.Maintain rapid, continuous rhythm between takeoff and landing.
3.Land on the balls of your feet, with heels briefly kissing the ground before immediately springing back up.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750671259100.jpg","context":"1.Keep your knees slightly bent, but relaxed."},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750671262859.jpg","context":"1.Control your landing and cushion the impact."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale short and even, inhale every two jumps if needed.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Bounce with your calves, ankles springing up and down.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Keep your knees stable and focus on quick ankle movement.

3.Hold the top position for 1–2 seconds, keeping your calves fully contracted.
